message:
fuzz(inode-roundtrip): add 13-byte all-zero seed for raw inode payload

Drop a single 13-byte all-zero input into the
`fuzz_inode_roundtrip` corpus directory. It pins the smallest
non-trivial raw payload — long enough that the roundtrip target
treats it as a real inode candidate (header + a few payload bytes)
yet short enough that libFuzzer's coverage-feedback loop can mutate
it cheaply on first run.

All-zero inputs are an effective seed for raw decoders: they exercise
the "all fields default" decode path that hand-written corpora often
miss because contributors gravitate to non-zero examples. Adding it
as a raw blob (rather than a structured Arbitrary case) keeps the
corpus directory canonical-format-agnostic, matching the style of
the rest of the seeds already shipped in this folder.

No code or harness changes — pure corpus addition that improves
first-iteration coverage for `fuzz_inode_roundtrip` without enlarging
the test surface or affecting CI runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
